title: Coordinate Plane
blurb: Placeholder — plot and identify points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane.

lessons.title: The Coordinate Plane
lessons.concreteComparison: Placeholder — real-world opener authored in Step 3. Example: a city map with numbered streets and avenues is like a coordinate grid.
lessons.objective: Placeholder — plot ordered pairs and identify quadrants on the coordinate plane.
concept: Placeholder concept paragraph. The coordinate plane is formed by a horizontal $x$-axis and a vertical $y$-axis intersecting at the origin $(0, 0)$.

workedExamples.statement: Placeholder problem. In which quadrant is the point $(-3, 4)$?

tryIt.statement: Placeholder try-it. Plot and name the quadrant for $(5, -2)$.

practice.statement: Placeholder practice problem. What is the distance between $(0, 0)$ and $(3, 4)$?

testYourself.statement: Placeholder test problem. Reflect the point $(2, -3)$ across the $x$-axis. What is the new point?
